strengths: Strong Python and advanced SQL proficiency
Hands-on ELT pipeline design with Airflow and dbt
Experience with BigQuery, GCS and Snowflake
Implemented data quality (Great Expectations) and CI/CD
Quantified impact on efficiency and data accuracy
weaknesses: Limited mention of data governance practices
Minimal large-scale data lake or Spark/Kafka experience
improvement_bullets: Architected and maintained scalable ETL/ELT pipelines within cloud data lakes, implementing strict data governance policies to ensure high-quality, compliant data distribution for downstream analytics.
Designed robust data architecture strategies by translating complex business requirements into technical solutions, utilizing star schema data modeling to optimize warehouse performance.
Integrated comprehensive data security practices, including IAM role management and encryption protocols, to secure sensitive datasets within GCS and BigQuery environments.
Led end-to-end data integration initiatives, bridging silos between relational databases and cloud environments to create a unified source of truth for stakeholder reporting.
Implemented data governance frameworks by documenting data lineage and establishing quality control rules, ensuring enterprise-wide adherence to security and regulatory standards.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer - Manager opportunity with PwC. I am interested in this opportunity because of PwC’s reputation for delivering transformative data solutions that drive actual business value for global clients. What interests me about working for PwC is its commitment to fostering a culture of innovation and continuous professional development, which resonates with my passion for staying at the cutting edge of cloud data engineering. I believe my expertise in building scalable ELT pipelines and my proficiency in cloud-based data architecture would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on both technical efficiency and business impact. In fact, at my previous project, I led the development of a production-grade ELT pipeline using Python, Airflow, and dbt to ingest complex economic data into BigQuery. I implemented automated data quality validation with Great Expectations and deployed CI/CD workflows via GitHub Actions. As a result, this solution reduced query latency and provided analysts with a reliable, high-performance dataset that improved reporting accuracy by 60%. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer - Manager role at PwC would allow me to bring my technical expertise in cloud infrastructure to support complex data architecture projects. Additionally, my role as an Analytics & Operations Lead at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my leadership and stakeholder management skills. I collaborated directly with leadership to define KPIs, translated business needs into actionable dashboards, and managed performance workflows that improved operational efficiency by 40%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and governance to contribute to PwC’s client delivery teams. I know that my technical versatility and commitment to high-quality data stewardship align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer - Manager role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
